title: श्री तुलसी माता आरती
devanagariText:
॥ श्री तुलसी माता आरती ॥

नमो नमो तुलसी महारानी, नमो नमो तुलसी महारानी।
मेरे स्वामीजी की तुम प्रिय ध्वजा, हरि की पटरानी॥

नमो नमो तुलसी महारानी॥

धन्य तुलसी पूरण तप कीनो, शालिग्राम बने हैं साथी।
धन्य तुलसी पूरण तप कीनो, शालिग्राम बने हैं साथी।
हरि के चरण कमल निज सीसे, द्वारा मैं पटरानी॥

नमो नमो तुलसी महारानी॥

कालिन्दी तट समीप विराजो, चारों धाम तिहारे।
कालिन्दी तट समीप विराजो, चारों धाम तिहारे।
सदा सुहागन सदा सुभागन, श्रीपति संग भवानी॥

नमो नमो तुलसी महारानी॥

जल तुलसी का हर पापी, विष्णु चरण मंगाये।
जल तुलसी का हर पापी, विष्णु चरण मंगाये।
सुख सम्पत्ति सब घर में आवे, शोभा अति कल्यानी॥

नमो नमो तुलसी महारानी॥

तुलसी तुम सब जीवन आधार, प्रेम से जो जन ध्यावे।
तुलसी तुम सब जीवन आधार, प्रेम से जो जन ध्यावे।
वृन्दा ठाकुरानी हरि प्रिया, भक्ति मुक्ति नित पावे॥

नमो नमो तुलसी महारानी॥

॥ इति श्री तुलसी माता आरती सम्पूर्ण ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Tulsi Mata Aarti ||

Namo Namo Tulsi Maharani, Namo Namo Tulsi Maharani.
Mere Swamiji Ki Tum Priya Dhwaja, Hari Ki Patrani.

Namo Namo Tulsi Maharani.

Dhanya Tulsi Puran Tap Keeno, Shaligram Bane Hain Saathi.
Dhanya Tulsi Puran Tap Keeno, Shaligram Bane Hain Saathi.
Hari Ke Charan Kamal Nij Seese, Dwara Main Patrani.

Namo Namo Tulsi Maharani.

Kalindi Tat Sameep Virajo, Charon Dham Tihare.
Kalindi Tat Sameep Virajo, Charon Dham Tihare.
Sada Suhagan Sada Subhagan, Shripati Sang Bhavani.

Namo Namo Tulsi Maharani.

Jal Tulsi Ka Har Paapi, Vishnu Charan Mangaye.
Jal Tulsi Ka Har Paapi, Vishnu Charan Mangaye.
Sukh Sampatti Sab Ghar Mein Aave, Shobha Ati Kalyani.

Namo Namo Tulsi Maharani.

Tulsi Tum Sab Jeevan Aadhaar, Prem Se Jo Jan Dhyave.
Tulsi Tum Sab Jeevan Aadhaar, Prem Se Jo Jan Dhyave.
Vrinda Thakurani Hari Priya, Bhakti Mukti Nit Pave.

Namo Namo Tulsi Maharani.

|| Iti Shri Tulsi Mata Aarti Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
नमो नमो तुलसी महारानी! मेरे स्वामीजी (विष्णु) की तुम प्रिय ध्वजा (चिह्न) हो, हरि की पटरानी हो।

धन्य हो तुलसी! पूर्ण तप किया, शालिग्राम तुम्हारे साथी बने। हरि के चरणकमल अपने शीश पर धरा, द्वार पर पटरानी बनकर विराजो।

कालिन्दी (यमुना) के तट समीप विराजमान हो, चारों धाम तुम्हारे हैं। सदा सुहागिन, सदा सौभाग्यशालिनी, श्रीपति (विष्णु) के संग हो।

तुलसी का जल हर पापी (मनुष्य) को विष्णु चरण प्रदान करता है। सुख-सम्पत्ति सब घर में आती है, अत्यन्त कल्याणी शोभा है।

तुलसी! तुम सब जीवन का आधार हो। प्रेम से जो जन ध्यान करे — हे वृन्दा ठकुरानी, हरिप्रिया! वह नित्य भक्ति-मुक्ति पाता है।
benefits: Traditional evening aarti of Tulsi Mata sung daily in Hindu households
Tulsi Vivah is one of the most important ceremonies in Kartik month
Traditionally associated with the presence of lord vishnu and lakshmi to the home
Invokes perpetual saubhagya (marital bliss) and prosperity
Tulsi's water purifies even the greatest sinners
Traditionally invoked to establish a sacred atmosphere and removes negativity from the home
description: Tulsi Aarti — श्री तुलसी माता आरती — is a traditional worship song (aarti) offered to Lord Vishnu. Rooted in Traditional Aarti Sangrah, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to create a sacred atmosphere and remove negativity from the home as well as grant perpetual saubhagya (marital bliss) and prosperity. Additionally, it is known to tulsi' water purifies even the greatest sinners. This prayer is traditionally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Tulsi Vivah, Devuthani Ekadashi, and Kartik Purnima to invoke divine grace. Comprising 5 verses, it can be completed in approximately 4 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Tulsi Aarti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Tulsi Aarti in Hindi with meaning - Namo Namo Tulsi Maharani. Traditional evening aarti for sacred Tulsi plant for Vishnu's blessings.

seoFaqs.question: Who is Tulsi Mata in the aarti?
seoFaqs.answer: Tulsi Mata is the divine personification of the holy basil (Ocimum sanctum), revered in the Vaishnava tradition as Vishnupriya, the beloved of Lord Vishnu. According to the Padma Purana and the Devi Bhagavata, Tulsi is the manifestation of Vrinda, a great devotee of Vishnu whose chastity and bhakti caused her to become the sacred plant after her death. Vishnu declared that he would always be worshipped together with Tulsi, and that no offering to him is complete without a Tulsi leaf. (Source: Drik Panchang; Webdunia; Divine Aarti.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between Tulsi Aarti and Tulsi Vivah?
seoFaqs.answer: Tulsi Vivah is the ceremonial marriage of the Tulsi plant (representing Vrinda Devi) to the Shaligram stone (representing Lord Vishnu), performed on Prabodhini Ekadashi (Dev Uthani Ekadashi) or on Kartik Shukla Dwadashi or Purnima. The Tulsi Aarti is the central devotional song of this ritual, sung to honour the bride Tulsi. The ceremony marks the auspicious end of Chaturmasya and the start of the Hindu marriage season. The aarti is also sung daily during Kartik for evening Tulsi puja. (Source: Drik Panchang; Bhakti Bharat; Amar Ujala.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Tulsi Aarti sung daily?
seoFaqs.answer: The Tulsi Aarti is traditionally sung at sunset, when devotees light a diya at the base of the Tulsi plant and circumambulate it. The whole month of Kartik (October-November) is considered the most auspicious period for daily Tulsi puja, with the aarti sung morning and evening. It is also sung on Vaishnava festivals such as Ekadashi, Janmashtami, Devotthana Ekadashi, and Akshaya Tritiya, and during the Chaturmasya vrat from Devshayani Ekadashi to Devotthana Ekadashi. (Source: Bhakti Bharat; Indif.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the Tulsi Aarti say about her powers?
seoFaqs.answer: The verses praise Tulsi as the giver of happiness to all the world (sab jag ki sukh data), the bestower of boons (vardata), the destroyer of disease and yoga obstacles, the savior of the fallen, the one who adorns Hari's head, and the granter of moksha to those who serve her. She is named the queen of all goddesses (Maharani), Hari's chief consort (Hari ki patrani), and the beloved of Krishna in his Vrindavan lila. The aarti integrates Vaishnava theology, Ayurvedic veneration of the plant, and ecological reverence. (Source: Times Now; Webdunia.)

seoFaqs.question: What rituals accompany the Tulsi Aarti?
seoFaqs.answer: Devotees water the Tulsi plant in the morning, offer raw cotton thread (sutra), sandalwood paste, turmeric, kumkum, and red bangles, light a ghee or sesame-oil diya at sunset, and circumambulate the plant 11 times while singing the aarti. Tulsi leaves are not plucked on Sundays, Ekadashis, or after sunset, and are gathered respectfully with both hands. During Tulsi Vivah, the plant is decorated as a bride with a chunari and bangles, and the Shaligram is placed beside her with full marriage rites. (Source: Divine Aarti; M Panchang.)
